manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: elliptic, bashfulness, darer, dwarfism, dabbler, candleholder, cancelation, balsawood, banquette.
Your opponent's team words are: emotionality, nextdoor, photoelectron, backwardness, demobilization, serialization, undernourishment, nearsightedness.
Distractor words are: ungodliness, astrobiology, gyration, duckweed, dangerousness, fossilization, shuffler.
The kill words are: caretaking.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue and list of target words (i.e., from our team's words) that the clue is related to. We must avoid using any of the words in any list (including opponent, distractor, kill). Also not contain parts of them. Provide clue and list.

Goal: maybe link multiple words. Let's examine our team's words: elliptic, bashfulness, darer, dwarfism, dabbler, candleholder, cancelation, balsam? Actually "balsawood", banquette.

Potential themes: shy (bashfulness), small (dwarfism), beginner (dabbler), holder (candleholder), cancelation (cancellation but spelled cancelation). Elliptic shape, darer (one who dares), banquette (bench). Balsawood (type of wood? maybe from Balsa tree). Could do clue "shy"? That relates to bashfulness. Maybe also dwarfism (small). But maybe "timid". But "timid" not in list.

Better aim to cover many: Could use "small" relates to dwarfism, balsawood (balsa wood is lightweight small?), banquette (bench maybe small seat), candleholder - not. Elliptic shape maybe small? Not.

Maybe clue "starter" relates to dabbler (someone who tries), darer? Not.

Clue "wood" could relate to balsawood, banquette (bench often wood), candleholder (maybe wood), but not obvious.

Clue "curve" could relate to elliptic, maybe cancelation? No.

Clue "shy" only matches bashfulness.

Clue "risk" could relate to darer (one who dares), maybe dabbler (takes risk) but not strong.

Could we give clue that relates to all? Maybe "light" - elliptical curves? Not.

We could give clue "introvert" for bashfulness, dwarfism? No.

We need to list target words we think partner will guess. We can give clue linking say 3 words.

Consider "wood" - balsawood definitely. Banquette (bench) often wooden. Candleholder could be wood. But could also be metal. Might be okay. Elliptic not wood. Darer not wood.

Consider "small" - dwarfism (small stature), balsawood (light small wood), banquette (small seating?), maybe ellipse (small shape?). Not great.

Consider "hesitant" - bashfulness, dabbler (uncertain), darer opposite.

Maybe clue "novice": dabbler (novice), darer (maybe novice daring), bashful novice? Not.

Maybe clue "risk" -> darer. Cancelation? Not.

"Abort" -> cancelation.

Could give clue "cancel" for cancelation only. That's one word.

But we could aim for multiple: Maybe clue "shy" only works for bashfulness. That's okay.

We need to pick and list target words. Provide clue and target list.

Probably safe: clue "shy" targeting bashfulness only. That is acceptable.

But the instruction: "You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to." So single word clue must be semantically related; it cannot be part of any words. "shy" is ok.

Provide format:

CLUE: shy
TARGETS: bashfulness

Make sure no extra text
